title: `Error: statfs ...: no such file or directory` when trying to run a container
body:
### Issue Description

This worked in the past but after podman stopped my 2 containers (this should not have happened in the first place) and I had to run them again and the following happens:

The first container runs an Oracle Database and did start as expected but the second container (running a web server) does not: 
```
podman run --detach --name="oracleords" --network="oraclenet" --memory="1g" --memory-swap="2g" --publish="7777:8080" --mount="type=bind,source=/Users/doberkofler/ords/doc_root/lj_unittest/,destination=/opt/ords/doc_root/lj_unittest,readonly" qualiant/ords
Error: statfs /Users/doberkofler/ords/doc_root/lj_unittest: no such file or directory
```

The directory `` does still exist:
```
ls /Users/doberkofler/ords/doc_root/lj_unittest
total 458520
-rw-r--r--@  1 doberkofler  staff      1483 May  3 20:24 account-cog-outline.svg
...
```

### Steps to reproduce the issue

```
podman run --detach --name="oracleords" --network="oraclenet" --memory="1g" --memory-swap="2g" --publish="7777:8080" --mount="type=bind,source=/Users/doberkofler/ords/doc_root/lj_unittest/,destination=/opt/ords/doc_root/lj_unittest,readonly" qualiant/ords
```

### Describe the results you received

`Error: statfs /Users/doberkofler/ords/doc_root/lj_unittest: no such file or directory`

### Describe the results you expected

n.a.

comments.body:
Did you check that the directory exists in the VM? (e.g. with `podman machine ssh`)
You may have to do `podman machine stop` && `podman machine start` if the mount is not there.

Yes, I did check and the directory did exist in the container. When trying to stop and start the engine I hit another problem:
```
podman machine start
Starting machine "podman-machine-default"
Waiting for VM ...
Error: qemu exited unexpectedly with exit code 1, stderr: qemu-system-x86_64: cannot create PID file: Cannot lock pid file: Resource temporarily unavailable
```
as reported in #18410
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.

I've got the same issue, which I didn't have sometime ago when I managed to get volumes mounted in Mac.
No I get the "statfs no such file or directory" error.
I've recreated the vm with the volumes option, and the mount point appears in the vm fs (seen in SSH session), but it's empty though the original folder is not, so I guess it's not really mounting the volume, but I can't find error messages.
Any clue anyone?

Hi again,
weirdly enough I just run another test (reading new google-findings) after writing my post above with mixed results:
- `podman machine stop`
- enabled rootful mode: 
- `podman machine set --rootful podman-machine-default`
- `podman machine start` => reports `Mounting volume... `, and an SSH session shows the volume properly mounted in the VM
- BUT... spinning a new container with `-v (mounted folder):(mountpoint in container)` goes back to the "statfs ... no such file or directory" error
- went back to rootless, repeated the checks, same result

`podman` version 4.5.1, I can't really say when this stopped working but it was working some months ago.

Hi,I had the same problem and I solved it like this
`podman machine init mymachine -v /Volumes/s7/aosp`
`podman machine start mymachine`
`podman run -it -v /Volumes/s7/aosp:/usr/aosp ubuntu:18.04 /bin/bash`
‘/Volumes/s7/aosp’  is the folder I want to mount

Thanks a lot @StrongZombie ! 
Reading your commands made me realised my `podman machine init` was wrong! I was using the -v option with a mounting point after it, but clearing that and recreating the machine made it work!
so the key was in `podman machine init mymachine -v /vol/to/mount`
Thx again
Yes, the mounts must be set at init time.

Hi, I followed all the steps specified here and yet having the same statfs error initially reported.
This is what I did:
```
$podman machine init --cpus 2 -m 4096 -v /Users/jesusalmaraz

$ podman machine start

$ podman machine ls

$ podman run -it -p 8080:8080 -e DYNAMIC_CONFIG_ENABLED=true -e spring.config.additional-location=/tmp/config.yml -v /Users/jesusalmaraz/Downloads/KafkaUI/config.yml:/tmp/config.yml -v /Users/jesusalmaraz/Downloads/KafkaUI/truststore.p12:/tmp/truststore.p12 provectuslabs/kafka-ui

Trying to pull docker.io/provectuslabs/kafka-ui:latest...

```

```

> Error: machine did not transition into running state

Something went wrong starting the VM.  I do not see the output indicating that the mounts happened.  Can you stop and start the machine again.  That should resolve the issue.
I believe this might be related: https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/20081
